global.title: Live Collaboration Track

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: Live collab via ACT + GSOC is the next major track — the decision that frames this MOC.
Architectural spine: per-user op feeds + GSOC pings + debounced pulls.
Specifies the ACT-gated collab manifest that lists participants.
Reactor's operation log handles concurrent edits natively — no CRDT — key simplifying decision.
UX scope: only the toolbar History view, not per-editor presence — bounds the build.
Collaborate tab in the chat panel is the live-collab UX entry point.
Collab index feed structure — data model the live track reads from.

global.description: Next-track design for live collaboration on Swarm: per-user op feeds, ACT-gated collab manifest, GSOC-triggered debounced pulls, no CRDT.
global.orientation: Live collaboration is the next major feature track after chat. This MOC consolidates the design notes: per-user operation feeds (not a shared feed, to avoid SOC write contention), ACT-gated collab manifests for participant discovery, and GSOC pings as the sub-second wake-up signal that triggers debounced pulls. The reactor's operation-based model handles concurrent edits natively, so no CRDT is needed. UX-wise the only surface is the document toolbar's History view — not per-editor presence — scoping the track tightly to what the primitives support.
